published.text:
### Core sections
Heading: AI automation for real estate — related links
Body: The broader real-estate parent page now points readers directly to the live first-project, lead-response, showing-coordination, and CRM-cleanup child pages instead of sending them only to generic cross-site workflow guides. The goal is to make the real-estate cluster easier to navigate as a bounded operating system.

Heading: What to automate first for real estate teams — related links
Body: The first-project page now links directly to the CRM cleanup and routing child alongside the broader parent, lead-response, and showing-coordination pages so the exact operational branch named in the page is also visible in the related-links block.

Heading: Real estate lead response and follow-up automation — cluster links
Body: The lead-response page now points to the real-estate first-project page, the showing-coordination child, and the CRM-cleanup child in both the proof-link area and the related-links block. This keeps readers inside the real-estate workflow cluster instead of pushing them out to generic pages too early.

Heading: Showing coordination automation for real estate teams — cluster links
Body: The showing-coordination page now points to the real-estate first-project page and the CRM-cleanup child in both the proof-link area and the related-links block. This makes the sibling workflow path clearer for buyers who realize the real bottleneck is upstream prioritization or CRM hygiene.

### Final CTA
Heading: Real-estate cluster navigation
Body: These link updates are meant to improve discovery and internal-path clarity, not change the offer itself. A reader who lands on one real-estate page should now have a cleaner path to the rest of the live cluster.

edits.description: Keep the tone operational and matter-of-fact.
Emphasize cluster clarity and bounded workflow navigation.
Do not present this as a new service or a new proof claim.
The change is maintenance-focused: better internal linking, not a new page ship.
